sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports a lack of sleep, which he attributes to travel and adjusting to the fall season. He has tried melatonin, meditation, and t- stretching every morning, but reports no improvement. He denies chest pain, shortness of breath, or any other concerning symptoms. He reports a lingering issue with his right knee following surgery, but otherwise states he has no additional weakness or issues.

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: Vital signs are stable. Physical examination reveals weakness of the lower extremities, with a 4/5 strength rating in the right leg and a 3/5 strength rating in the left leg. Reflexes are intact. The right knee x-ray demonstrates arthritic changes. Lung sounds are clear bilaterally.

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: The patient's primary concern is insomnia, likely related to stress and travel. He has a history of osteoporosis and multiple sclerosis. Examination reveals weakness in the lower extremities and arthritic changes in the right knee.
medical_concepts: arthritic changes in the right knee

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: Order a refill of fosamax 1 tablet per week, 11 refills. Continue the patient on current multiple sclerosis medications and schedule a follow-up neurology appointment. Arrange for a referral to physical therapy for evaluation of the right knee and lower extremity weakness.

medical_concepts: arthritic changes in the right knee
